text:
an arbitrator has power to grant pre-reference interest 
under the Interest Act, 1978 as well as pendente lite and 
future interest. However, he is constricted only by the 
fact that an agreement between the parties may contain 
an express bar to the award of pre-reference and/or 
pendente lite interest. Since interest is compensatory in 
nature and is parasitic upon a principal sum not having 
been paid in time, this Court has frowned upon clauses 
that bar the payment of interest. It has therefore evolved 
the test of strict construction of such clauses, and has 
gone on to state that unless there is a clear and express 
bar to the payment of interest that can be awarded by 
an arbitrator, clauses which do not refer to claims before 
the arbitrators or disputes between parties and clearly 
bar payment of interest, cannot stand in the way of an 
arbitrator awarding pre-reference or pendente lite interest.
